title: 10 best herbal creams for nausea

remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint oil has natural digestive stimulating properties that help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by allowing for better digestion.
Anxiolytic: The calming effects of peppermint oil have a mild anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effect, which can help reduce stress and anxiety-related nausea and discomfort.
Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation that may be contributing to nausea and vomiting.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract. The menthol in peppermint cream stimulates digestion, reducing symptoms of nausea and vomiting. The cooling sensation of peppermint also helps to calm the stomach and alleviate discomfort associated with nausea. As a result, peppermint creams have become a popular remedy for nausea and digestive issues, providing relief and soothing the stomach.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps alleviate nausea by numbing the stomach and reducing inflammation, providing fast relief from nausea and vomiting.
Menthone: Menthone acts as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus and other irritants from the stomach, reducing nausea and promoting digestion.

Limonene (specifically beta-pinene and beta-myrcene): Limonene, a terpene found in peppermint oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea by calming the stomach and alleviating digestive discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Rhyzomes: Utilized for their robust peppermint flavor and aroma, which helps to ease nausea and promote digestion.
Stems: Employed for their menthol content, which provides a cooling sensation that can help soothe an upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in 500ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the herbal liquid and discard the plant material to obtain a clear solution.
Mix 20g of candelilla wax with 10g of shea butter and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine 20ml of the infused herbal liquid with 10g of aloe vera gel and 5g of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture from steps 3 and 4 into a mold and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger's natural digestive stimulant properties help to increase stomach contractions, which can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting by moving food through the digestive system more efficiently.

Anti-Inflammatory: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting, and also help to reduce the severity of symptoms associated with motion sickness and morning sickness.
Antioxidant: Ginger's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage stomach lining and exacerbate nausea, while also supporting overall gut health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to stimulate digestion. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. These properties have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various stomach-related issues, including morning sickness, motion sickness, and nausea associated with chemotherapy. The topical application of ginger creams allows for direct absorption into the skin, providing quick relief from nausea.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol is a bioactive compound that helps alleviate nausea by inhibiting the activity of the neurotransmitter serotonin, a key player in nausea and vomiting.

Shogaol: Shogaol is a compound that has been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce nausea by blocking the action of serotonin on the vagus nerve, which is responsible for nausea and vomiting.

6-Gingerol: 6-Gingerol is a compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce nausea by reducing inflammation and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters involved in nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Roots: Roots are also widely used as they contain a similar concentration of gingerols and shogaols as rhyzomes, making them effective in reducing nausea.
Buds: Buds are sometimes used due to their higher concentration of shogaols, which have been shown to have a more potent anti-emetic effect compared to gingerols.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of fresh zingiber officinale root in 2 cups of carrier oil at 70-80 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the infused oil with the beeswax mixture and stir until it thickens.
Pour the mixture into glass containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon balm creams contain antioxidants like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea.
GABA-nergic activity: Lemon balm has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which helps calm the nervous system and relieve nausea and vomiting.
Digestive soothing compounds: Lemon balm contains volatile oils that help soothe digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea, which can contribute to nausea.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties. The active compounds in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and linalool, have natural anti-inflammatory and sedative effects, which can help to ease nausea and vomiting. These compounds also have a positive impact on the digestive system, helping to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making lemon balm creams a popular natural remedy for nausea and other digestive issues. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps alleviate nausea by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach.
Linalool: A terpene that helps with nausea by acting on the central nervous system to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by stress and anxiety.
Limonene: A terpene that helps with nausea by stimulating digestive enzymes and improving gut motility, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly linalool and citral, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Flowers: Used for their fragrance and oil content, which helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea.
Roots: Contain a higher concentration of certain volatile oils than the leaves, contributing to their use in nausea-relieving creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of melissa officinalis leaves from the plant and dry them in a warm place for 24 hours.
Combine 50g of the dried melissa officinalis leaves with 500ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours and then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with 100ml of the infused oil in a separate bowl.
Heat the mixture of beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of nausea.
Sedative and calming effects: Chamomile's sedative properties can help calm the stomach and relax the muscles, reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort associated with nausea.
Antispasmodic activity: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds, such as flavonoids, help relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea, bloating, and abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile have a relaxing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and calming the stomach muscles. This can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues, motion sickness, and other conditions. Additionally, chamomile's ability to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm can also contribute to its effectiveness in managing nausea. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with nausea by acting as a natural anxiolytic and sedative, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with anxiety and stress.
Apollinarin: This flavone glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by inflammation and muscle spasms in the digestive tract.

Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing smooth muscle in the digestive tract and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high apigenin and luteolin content, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate nausea.
Leaves: They are used for their flavonoids, particularly apigenin, which helps in reducing inflammation and alleviating nausea symptoms.
Seeds: They are used due to their high concentration of apigenin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help in treating nausea and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 100ml of carrier oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt for 10 minutes.
Add 30ml of the infused oil and 10ml of distilled water to the melted wax mixture.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean container and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Fennel's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to nausea.
Carminative property: Fennel's carminative property helps to expel gas from the stomach and intestines, reducing bloating and discomfort that can exacerbate nausea.
Antioxidant property: Fennel's antioxidant property helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and other symptoms of indigestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with nausea because it contains compounds like anethole and fenchone, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to relax the digestive system and reduce spasms in the stomach, alleviating nausea and vomiting. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to release trapped gas in the digestive tract, further relieving nausea and discomfort. As a result, fennel creams have become a popular natural remedy for nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone is a monoterpene alcohol that helps alleviate nausea by inhibiting the activity of cholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down neurotransmitters involved in nausea and vomiting.

Anethole: Anethone is an aromatic ether that helps reduce nausea by acting on the central nervous system to decrease the sensitivity of chemoreceptors in the brain, which are responsible for triggering nausea and vomiting.

Foeniculin: Foeniculin is a sesquiterpene alcohol that has anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce nausea by stabilizing the stomach and reducing the movement of gastric contents, which can cause nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has a calming effect and helps reduce nausea.
Seeds: The seeds of Fennel contain estragole and fenchone, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Stems: The stems of Fennel contain similar volatile oils as the leaves, which can also help reduce nausea and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh foeniculum vulgare roots in the morning for maximum potency and antioxidant properties.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours to preserve bioactive compounds.
Grind 200g of dried foeniculum vulgare roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100g of beeswax and 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes at 180f to create a smooth and consistent herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Dill cream's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with nausea and vomiting.

Carminative property: The carminative properties of dill cream help to reduce the formation of gas in the stomach, which can contribute to nausea and bloating, allowing for easier digestion and relief from discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory property: Dill cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anethum graveolens, also known as dill, creams helps with nausea because of its unique properties. Dill contains a compound called carvone, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. These properties help to soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea. Additionally, dill's fragrance can help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort. By applying dill cream to the skin, individuals can experience rapid relief from nausea and enjoy a more comfortable and peaceful state.
remedy_constituents: Dill terpenes: These volatile compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system.

Apiol: Apiol is a phenolic compound found in dill that has been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting. Its anti-emetic properties are thought to be responsible for its ability to help alleviate nausea.

Dill essential oil's monoterpenes (such as d-limonene): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting by modulating the gut-brain axis and reducing inflammation in the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative properties, which help to release trapped gas in the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
Leaves: They are used because of their antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea.
Fruits: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of dill weed leaves and flowers for the desired amount of anethum graveolens cream.
Dry the dill weed leaves and flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Infuse 1 cup of dried dill weed in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate the oil from the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative Properties: Lavender's calming effects can help alleviate nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which is often a contributing factor to nausea.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting by soothing and protecting the mucous membranes.

remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their calming and soothing properties, which help to reduce nausea and anxiety.
Flowers: The essential oils extracted from the flowers have a sedative effect, which can help to alleviate nausea and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried flowers in a carrier oil such as jojoba oil at a 1:3 ratio for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of emulsifying wax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for several hours before transferring it to a jar.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow herb contains a thick, gel-like substance called mucilage, which forms a protective coating in the stomach and intestines, helping to soothe and calm irritation, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with nausea.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of nausea, such as bloating and cramping.

By applying the cream topically, it can help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce the sensation of nausea, promoting a sense of relief and calmness. remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with nausea by forming a protective, soothing barrier on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to nausea.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids, such as triterpenoid saponins, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate nausea by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help ease nausea by reducing inflammation and spasms in the stomach, promoting digestive comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part, as they contain high concentrations of mucilages, which provide soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are also used to make creams for nausea, as they contain similar mucilages and other compounds that help to calm the digestive system.

Barks: The barks of the Althaea officinalis plant contain mucilages and other compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system, making them a useful ingredient in creams for nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of clean dried roots of althaea officinalis in late summer or early fall.
Infuse 2 cups of distilled water with 1/2 cup of dried roots for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth to separate roots from liquid and discard the solids immediately.
Combine 1/2 cup of infused liquid with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted and the cream has thickened.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, leading to a decrease in symptoms such as stomach upset and queasiness.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice creams have antioxidant properties that help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by digestive acids, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with nausea.

Isoflavans: Isoflavans in licorice creams exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of nausea such as bloating and stomach cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with nausea because it contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties may help soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation that can lead to nausea. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of nausea. Its anti-emetic properties may help stabilize the stomach and reduce the frequency and severity of nausea episodes. This makes licorice creams a potential natural remedy for nausea.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce nausea by stabilizing stomach acid and alleviating gastrointestinal irritation.
Liquiritigenin: This flavanone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea caused by gastrointestinal disorders.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe nausea.
Leaves: High in flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea symptoms.
Barks: Contain glycosides and polyphenols that can help calm the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200g of dried roots using a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio.
Combine the extracted liquid with 100ml of distilled water and heat it gently to 60-70°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities and obtain a clear liquid.
Mix 1g of beeswax with 10g of candelilla wax and 10g of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the herbal extract with the wax mixture and emulsify it using a homogenizer or blender for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Thujone: Thujone, a primary constituent of wormwood, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in wormwood, such as camphor, borneol, and terpenes, have natural analgesic and antiseptic properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea symptoms.

Bitters: Wormwood contains bitters, which stimulate digestion and improve gut motility by increasing the production of digestive enzymes and hormones, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with poor digestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, creams helps with nausea because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, particularly thujone and borneol. These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the stomach and calm the digestive system. The creams also contain camphor, which can help to relax the muscles and reduce cramping. remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce nausea and vomiting by alleviating underlying inflammation and pain.
Thujone: A terpenic ketone, thujone has been reported to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with anxiety or stress.

Artabsin: A sesquiterpene lactone, artabsin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce nausea and vomiting by mitigating oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in relieving nausea and other digestive issues.
Stems: They are used for their bitter compounds, such as thujone, which aid in reducing nausea and other gastrointestinal problems.
Roots: They are used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to calm the stomach and relieve nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers on a dry sunny day.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 30 minutes.
Infuse 1 cup of the dried plant material in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at 180°f for 10 minutes then pour it into small containers to cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pennyroyal creams helps with nausea because they contain menthol, a natural compound that stimulates the digestive system and reduces stomach contractions. When applied topically to the skin, menthol increases blood flow and relieves pressure on the stomach, helping to alleviate feelings of queasiness and vomiting. Additionally, pennyroyal's antispasmodic properties help to calm the muscles in the abdominal area, further reducing nausea and discomfort. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Pennyroyal's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea.

Carminative properties: Pennyroyal's carminative properties help to release trapped gas from the digestive system, which can alleviate nausea and bloating associated with indigestion and other gastrointestinal issues.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pennyroyal plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly menthol and menthone, which help to soothe and calm the stomach, making it effective in treating nausea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves at dawn and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for several hours.
Combine 30 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of distilled water and steep for 15 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids after pressing firmly on the cloth.
Mix 20 grams of petroleum jelly or beeswax with 80 milliliters of the peppermint tea and heat in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 2 drops of vitamin e oil and pour mixture into sterilized glass jars for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with nausea because they contain extracts of the herb Angelica archangelica, which has been used for centuries to alleviate digestive issues. The cream's active compounds, such as angelic acid and isoimperatorin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with nausea. Additionally, Angelica creams can also calm the mind and body, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility that helps alleviate queasiness and vomiting.
remedy_properties: Angelica archangelica's anti-inflammatory compounds: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

Choline-containing compounds: Angelica contains choline, a precursor to the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, which plays a key role in regulating muscle contractions and relaxations. This helps alleviate spasms that can cause nausea and vomiting.

Essential oils (such as angelicin and apiol): These essential oils have been shown to have anti-emetic properties, meaning they help relieve or prevent nausea and vomiting. They work by stabilizing the digestive system and reducing stomach contractions that can contribute to nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and coumarins, which help to calm stomach upset and reduce nausea.
Rhyzomes: Containing volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe digestive discomfort and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried angelica archangelica root, ensuring it's free from contaminants and debris.
Combine the dried roots with 4 tablespoons of sweet almond oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes over low heat, allowing the ingredients to infuse and emulsify slowly.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with nausea because of its unique properties that work in harmony to soothe digestive issues. The cream's active compound, cinnamaldehyde, has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon is known for its ability to relax muscles and calm the nervous system, providing relief from nausea and vomiting episodes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can alleviate nausea caused by inflammation or gut irritation.

Gastrointestinal soothing properties: The cinnamic acid present in Ceylon cinnamon has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that help calm the digestive tract, reducing nausea and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), motion sickness, and morning sickness.

Carminative effect: The volatile oils in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamyl acetate, have a carminative property that helps to release trapped gas in the stomach, reducing nausea and bloating caused by digestive issues like indigestion and constipation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is a good source of cinnamaldehyde, a compound responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea.
Barks: The inner bark of the Ceylon cinnamon plant contains essential oils and compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry cinnamomum verum bark in the shade for 7-10 days to preserve its potency.
Grind 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered cinnamomum verum with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts, then remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture into a smooth cream using a hand mixer for 2-3 minutes, yielding 150 milliliters of finished cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with nausea because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work synergistically to soothe and calm the digestive system. The cream's active ingredients, including Ginkgo biloba and Peppermint oil, help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the stomach, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the cream's gentle, non-irritating properties make it an excellent option for sensitive skin, allowing it to provide targeted relief without causing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation in the gut, which may contribute to nausea relief.
Stomach-soothing compounds: The plant contains flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that may help calm digestive issues, reducing nausea and discomfort.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve circulation, which could alleviate nausea by reducing pressure on the stomach and gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving nausea symptoms.
Barks: The barks contain phenolic compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to soothe stomach upset and calm nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves from trusted sources in sufficient quantities for processing.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or desiccant machines to reduce moisture content within 24 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix one tablespoon of ground ginkgo biloba powder with two tablespoons of coconut oil and four tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency, then pour it into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set within 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the digestive system. The cream's active ingredients, such as valeric acid and isovaleric acid, work to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting. This can be especially helpful for individuals experiencing motion sickness, pregnancy-related nausea, or chemotherapy-induced nausea. By soothing the digestive system, valerian creams provide relief from discomfort and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a key constituent of valerian root, has been shown to have anti-nausea effects by blocking the activity of neurotransmitters that trigger nausea and vomiting.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, another main component of valerian root, has been found to have antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve muscle cramps and spasms in the stomach and intestines that often contribute to nausea.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in valerian cream, such as borneol and Bornyl acetate, have been shown to have sedative and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a unique combination of terpenes, including valerenic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours then grind into powder.
Mix 20g of dried powdered valeriana officinalis with 100ml of sweet almond oil and let it infuse for 8 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and combine with 10g of beeswax and 20ml of coconut oil then melt in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Pour the melted cream mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool to room temperature before sealing tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with nausea because they contain properties that have a calming effect on the stomach and digestive system. The active compounds in maypop, such as alkaloids and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscle contractions, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the cream's topical application allows for rapid absorption into the bloodstream, providing quick relief from discomfort and reducing the severity of nausea-related distress.
remedy_properties: Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata): Contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes that have anti-anxiety and sedative effects, helping to calm the stomach and reduce nausea.
Vitex Agnus-castus (Chaste Tree Berry): Exhibits antispasmodic properties, which help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to nausea.
Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antinausea properties, helping to reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant are used due to their ability to soothe digestive issues and calm stomach discomfort, making them an effective ingredient in creams for nausea relief.
Barks: The barks of the Maypop plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality passiflora incarnata flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when fragrant.
Combine one cup of dried flowers with three cups of coconut oil and one teaspoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid infusion.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to an airtight jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties in sage help to soothe the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, sage's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramping and spasms that can exacerbate nausea. As a result, using herbal sage creams can provide quick and effective relief from nausea-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating nausea symptoms.
Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Gastroprotective compounds: Sage contains compounds that have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage, reducing inflammation and irritation, and helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain bioactive compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective against nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of salvia officinalis from your garden or purchase from a reputable supplier, ensuring 20 grams of high-quality material.
Combine the dried salvia leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass container, allowing it to steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and retaining the infused oil, which will be used as the base of your cream.

Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the infused oil, gently heating the mixture in a double boiler until the wax has melted and the temperature reaches 180 degrees fahrenheit.

Remove the pot from heat, let it cool slightly, then whip the cream with an electric mixer or whisk until it thickens and emulsifies into a smooth, consistent consistency suitable for application to the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with nausea because of its unique ability to calm the stomach and alleviate digestive discomfort. The natural oils and compounds found in yarrow, such as chamazulene and apigenin, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to reduce inflammation and cramping in the digestive tract. This relief can bring rapid and significant relief from nausea and vomiting, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals experiencing nausea due to various causes, including motion sickness, morning sickness, and food poisoning.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow cream can help to relax spasms in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to nausea by reducing muscle contractions that can cause discomfort and queasiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with inflammatory conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Astringent properties: Yarrow cream has astringent properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated stomach tissue, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to nausea and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate nausea.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing inflammation associated with nausea.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried achillea millefolium flowers and other herbs such as peppermint and ginger for added benefits.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried herb mixture and combine it with two tablespoons of olive oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat for 10-15 minutes stirring occasionally until the oil is infused.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax to the cooled herbal infusion and stir until fully melted then pour the cream into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound creams helps with nausea because of its natural antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the stomach and digestive tract. The cream's active ingredients, including licorice root and ginger, work together to calm the muscles in the abdomen and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms such as queasiness and discomfort. Additionally, horehound's sweet and soothing aroma can also provide emotional comfort and relaxation, further contributing to its effectiveness in combating nausea.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Herbal horehound creams contain compounds like marrubiin, which stimulate digestive enzymes and improve digestion, helping to alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining.

Antispasmodic Properties: Horehound's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea and vomiting, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced discomfort.

Expectorant and Anti-Inflammatory: Horehound's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycosides and flavonoids, the roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing nausea.
Barks: Containing triterpenoids, the barks have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marrubium vulgare leaves from your garden or local foraging spot, ensuring at least 2 ounces are collected.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set at 95°f for 4 hours or air-drying them for 7 days.
Crush 1 ounce of dried marrubium vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, then sift through a coarse-mesh strainer.
Combine the powdered marrubium vulgare with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax has melted and the cream has cooled to 180°f, then pour into sterilized glass containers for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for nausea are topical products infused with natural ingredients, such as peppermint, ginger, and chamomile, which help alleviate nausea and vomiting. These creams work by stimulating the senses and promoting relaxation, thereby reducing nausea and discomfort. Examples include creams with ginger and peppermint, which have been shown to provide relief from morning sickness and chemotherapy-induced nausea. This can greatly improve lives by providing a natural alternative to medication and allowing individuals to maintain their daily routines.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal creams are digestive issues such as indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile can provide relief from these conditions. Additionally, herbal creams may also be used to treat skin irritations and inflammation, such as eczema and acne, which can exhibit symptoms similar to nausea.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with nausea is a blend of ginger and peppermint. Ginger cream has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, while peppermint cream helps to calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. Adding a small amount of chamomile cream can also help to relax and calm the mind, further reducing the likelihood of nausea. These creams can be applied topically to the skin or taken orally as a supplement.
